Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First the root is a SEQUENCE so the order of top level activities is 1 Receive Order 2 Check Inventory 3 Then the xor node 4 Then Close Order

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The xor nodes children are the successful subtree parallel of auth pay package ship and the cancel subtree sequence of cancel then close

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The cancel subtree already has a Close Order inside it and the root also has a Close Order after the xor node

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If we pick the cancel path the xor does Cancel Order then Close Order then the root does another Close Order so that trace would be Receive Check Cancel Close Close

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The successful subtree is parallel of Auth Pay Package Ship so those three can be executed in any order

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The model allows Ship Order before Authorize Payment and before Package Items because it places them in parallel with no ordering constraints

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The trace Receive Order Check Inventory Ship Order Package Items Authorize Payment Close Order is allowed by the model

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The root sequence is satisfied by Receive Order then Check Inventory then the xor picks the successful subtree and the parallel subtree is satisfied because Ship Package Auth are all present and then Close Order is last

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a real Order to Cash process you must authorize payment before packaging or shipping and you must package before shipping so Ship Order before Package Items and before Authorize Payment violates the natural order of operations

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: The duplicate Close trace Receive Order Check Inventory Cancel Order Close Order Close Order is also an invalid trace accepted by the model because an order cannot be closed twice
